item.title: Court told Kamikamica’s affidavit did not add up with other evidence
item.description: The first FICAC witness in the case of former Deputy Prime Minister Manoa Kamikamica testified in court today that after the Commission of Inquiry received his affidavit, some things did not add up. Janet Mason who was the counsel assisting the Commission of Inquiry says that what was in Kamikamica’s affidavit did not match up to what other respondents had stated.
item.content:encoded:
The first FICAC witness in the case of former Deputy Prime Minister Manoa Kamikamica testified in court today that after the Commission of Inquiry received his affidavit, some things did not add up.

Janet Mason who was the counsel assisting the Commission of Inquiry says that what was in Kamikamica’s affidavit did not match up to what other respondents had stated.

Mason said in her cross examination in chief that Kamikamica did not state his relationship with former FICAC Commissioner Barbara Malimali on when he knew she was getting appointed as well as his interaction with Frances Puleiwai.

She further says that according to Government MP Kalaveti Ravu, Kamikamica approached him three times.

The witness says on the first occasion Kamikamica asked Ravu about his case, on the second if he knew that Malimali was going to be appointed FICAC Commissioner, while on the third occasion Ravu said that Kamikamica told him that if Malimali becomes Commissioner they can see FICAC about closing his case.

In the COI transcripts, when Kamikamica was asked if he spoke to Ravu he said he had asked about his case but had not said anything about Malimali or known of her appointment as FICAC Commissioner until after it was announced.

Mason is still being cross examined by defence counsel Wylie Clarke

Kamikamica is charged by FICAC for perjury and giving false information to a public servant.

item.title: Local Emmy Award nominee Lanza Coffin to appear on fijivillage Straight Talk at 7pm tomorrow

item.title: Dream of home ownership becomes reality for 31 families at Nepani
item.description: The dream of owning a home has become a reality for 31 families after they received the keys to their new homes at the Housing Authority's Nepani Subdivision today. While officiating at the handover ceremony, Prime Minister, Sitiveni Rabuka congratulated the new homeowners and encouraged them to build safe and loving homes where their children can grow and thrive.
item.content:encoded:
The dream of owning a home has become a reality for 31 families after they received the keys to their new homes at the Housing Authority's Nepani Subdivision today.

While officiating at the handover ceremony, Prime Minister, Sitiveni Rabuka congratulated the new homeowners and encouraged them to build safe and loving homes where their children can grow and thrive.

Rabuka acknowledged the role the Housing Authority in helping families achieve home ownership, saying it gives them the opportunity to build a better future.

He also commended Minister for Housing and Local Government Maciu Nalumisa for exploring successful affordable housing models overseas that could benefit Fiji.

Rabuka highlighted examples from overseas where surplus building materials are used to build homes for vulnerable people, including former offenders, people recovering from drug addiction and those experiencing homelessness, giving them a fresh start in life.

He urged the 31 new homeowners to cherish their homes, support one another and provide a secure environment for their families and future generations.

Rabuka shared his own family's journey to home ownership, recalling how his parents bought a piece of land in Nabua in the late 1950s and spent almost 10 years paying it off before securing a Housing Authority "Borrow and Build" loan of $5,400 to construct their family home.

He says the family built the house themselves, mixing concrete, making concrete blocks and laying the foundation by hand, adding that the home still stands today.

The Prime Minister also revealed that he later paid off the Housing Authority loan after returning from peacekeeping duties in Lebanon in 1981.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: FTUC warns of national march over Employment Relations Bill delay
item.description: The Fiji Trades Union Congress has warned it may organise a national march if the long-delayed Employment Relations Bill continues to stall in Parliament. The warning follows an urgent FTUC National Council meeting held on Saturday , where members raised concerns over delays in progressing the Bill, sectoral wages council orders, the National Minimum Wage and the proposed National Living Wage.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Trades Union Congress has warned it may organise a national march if the long-delayed Employment Relations Bill continues to stall in Parliament.

The warning follows an urgent FTUC National Council meeting held on Saturday , where members raised concerns over delays in progressing the Bill, sectoral wages council orders, the National Minimum Wage and the proposed National Living Wage.

FTUC National Secretary Felix Anthony says workers have been waiting for Government commitments to be fulfilled for the past three years, with reforms to the Employment Relations Act pending for more than 12 years.

Minister for Employment, Productivity and Workplace Relations Agni Deo Singh was also present at the meeting to clarify the Government's position on the outstanding issues.

Anthony says the Minister assured the Council that the Employment Relations Bill will be presented during the August sitting of Parliament and that the Government remains committed to enacting the legislation.

He says the Minister informed members that the appointment of Wages Council members will be completed within a month, after the terms of the previous tripartite council members expired.

The FTUC says it welcomes the Government's commitment to develop a transparent approach to revising the National Minimum Wage and establishing the National Living Wage, with funding allocated in the 2026-2027 National Budget for the process.

The Council also raised concerns regarding the reduction of employer contributions to the Fiji National Provident Fund.

Anthony says the Minister indicated that he hopes the contribution rate will be restored soon.

The FTUC is calling on the Government to prioritise the Employment Relations Bill, saying it aligns with recommendations made by the International Labour Organisation's Committee of Experts.

A nthony says the union movement is still awaiting the Government's commitment to ensure the FNPF Board composition becomes tripartite.

item.title: CFL thanks loyal listeners and online followers with free Gratitude Concert at Albert Park this Saturday
item.description: Communications Fiji Limited will celebrate 41 years of connecting with listeners and communities with a free, family-friendly Gratitude Concert at Albert Park in Suva this Saturday. CFL General Manager Charles Taylor says the concert was born out of the company's 40th anniversary celebrations last year and was created as a way of giving back to loyal listeners and online followers who have support
item.content:encoded:
Communications Fiji Limited will celebrate 41 years of connecting with listeners and communities with a free, family-friendly Gratitude Concert at Albert Park in Suva this Saturday.

CFL General Manager Charles Taylor says the concert was born out of the company's 40th anniversary celebrations last year and was created as a way of giving back to loyal listeners and online followers who have supported CFL over the years.

Insert: Chas Taylor - listen to some great music July 21

Taylor says the concert will feature a line-up of local artists, including Sweet Sensation, Seru Serevi, The Gang and Ju Ben, with more performers to be announced.

He says the event is designed for the whole family, encouraging people to bring their loved ones and a mat to enjoy an afternoon of music and entertainment at Albert Park.

The event will begin at 1pm, with food stalls opening for families, while live performances will start at 3pm.

Families will also have access to food stalls and children's activities, including bouncy castles, creating a safe and enjoyable environment for everyone.

Taylor also confirmed that singer Georgina Ledua, who was expected to perform, will no longer be able to attend due to flight arrangements.

However, Aggie Vakaloloma has stepped in and will perform at the concert.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Taveuni Buses Limited to resume services this afternoon, thanks residents for helping students
item.description: Speaking to fijivillage News, Managing Director Sami Prakash apologised to students, parents and schools for the disruption caused over the past two days. Prakash also thanked the residents and businesses who stepped in to drop students, while school bus services were suspended.
item.content:encoded:
Speaking to fijivillage News, Managing Director Sami Prakash apologised to students, parents and schools for the disruption caused over the past two days.

Prakash also thanked the residents and businesses who stepped in to drop students, while school bus services were suspended.

He says members of the community, including business owners and residents who volunteered their vehicles, played an important role in ensuring many students, particularly those sitting exams, were able to get to and from school during the disruption.

Prakash says the company appreciates the support shown by the community and thanked everyone who assisted while services were suspended.

He assured parents and schools that Taveuni Buses will continue providing safe and reliable transport for students across the island.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Trump signs order imposing 50% tariff on Canadian imports
item.description: US President Donald Trump has signed an executive order imposing a 50 percent tariff on a wide range of goods imported from Canada

item.title: Rabuka confirms elections will proceed as planned
item.description: Fiji will go to the polls whether or not a new or amended Constitution is in place.That is the message from Prime Minister Sitiven
